html:
Exclusive to:

Contents

  • 1 Reinforced Support Pillar
  • 2 Reinforced Dreadstone Pillar
  • 3 Trivia
  • 4 Gallery

Reinforced Support Pillar

Support PillarDamaged Support PillarSupport Pillar ScaffoldSupport Pillar Wreckage

Reinforced Support Pillars are needed to protect the area from falling objects during an earthquake. Initially, you need to craft a Pillar Scaffold, and then put 40 Rocks into it. After they've been fully built or repaired, Pillars withstand 10 Earthquakes before losing durability. The Pillars lose 1 durability for every earthquake it prevents from dropping falling debris around itself, which will show visibly on the Pillar as big cracks. The player can choose to repair it by inserting Rocks into it, with the amount depending on how damaged the pillar is. If a Shadow Rift is currently active, it will also block Cave-in Boulders for no extra cost.

When hit by a Hammer, each hit results in 1 Earthquake of durability loss, which means that from full durability, it takes 50 hits to fully break a Support Pillar.

Reinforced Dreadstone Pillar

Dreadstone PillarDamaged Dreadstone PillarDreadstone Pillar ScaffoldDreadstone Pillar Wreckage

Reinforced Dreadstone Pillars are obtained by learning one of the blueprints dropped from Nightmare Werepig, after defeating the Ancient Guardian. Dreadstone Pillars work similarly to Support Pillars, but are much more resilient as they can regenerate their durability every 2 days. It has the same 10 Earthquake grace period before it starts cracking, which is also regenerated over time. Realistically, the player would not need to repair it manually by inserting Dreadstone into it, as it would regenerate enough while it's not withstanding earthquakes, but if the Dreadstone Pillar is ever damaged enough for it to be destroyed, the player will need to repair it by inserting 40 Dreadstones, as in this stage it does not regenerate durability.

While the Dreadstone Pillar is in a damaged state and repairing itself, it has a negative Sanity aura within a radius of 8 wall units, with the intensity of the drain depending on the player's distance to the Pillar. The maximum sanity drain from its aura when standing right next to is around -10/min.

For the Dreadstone Pillar to be broken by a player, a regular hammer is not sufficient. It can only be broken by a Brightshade Smasher or Woodie with the The Werebeaver III Skill. Each hit results in 1 Earthquake of durability loss. From full durability, it takes 50 hits with the Smasher or Werebeaver for the Dreadstone Pillar to break. The Dreadstone Pillar Wreckage left behind when it breaks also requires the aforementioned tools to be destroyed.

Trivia

  • The Reinforced Support Pillar was introduced in the Skill Spotlight: Wormwood, Wolfgang, Woodie update.
  • Before update 567776, they did not have a 90° model, and the Scaffold Pillar model did not have stones on the floor

Support PillarDamaged Support PillarSupport Pillar ScaffoldSupport Pillar Wreckage
“It fills me with confidence.”
–Wilson
“It's nice not having to worry about rocks falling on my head.”
–Willow
“Is mighty pillar!”
–Wolfgang
“It promises protection, but for how long?”
–Wendy
“A TOWERING MONUMENT TO MY GREATNESS”
–WX-78
“That should stabilize the cave's ceiling for now.”
–Wickerbottom
“Tall and sturdy as a redwood.”
–Woodie
“It will hold, for now.”
–Maxwell
“A glöriöus mönument tö öur resilience!”
–Wigfrid
“It'll keep us safe!”
–Webber
“It's nice to have some stability.”
–Warly
“Ahhh. Safer now”
–Wormwood
“Good and sturdy!”
–Winona
“Thank you for your support, hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Made with own two claws!”
–Wurt
“That's not going anywhere!”
–Walter
“I wonder how long it will last.”
–Wanda
“I should really get around to fixing that.”
–Wilson
“I don't wanna be here when that thing finally breaks.”
–Willow
“Stay strong, mighty pillar!”
–Wolfgang
“The cracks have started to show...”
–Wendy
“STRUCTURAL INTEGRITY HAS BEEN COMPROMISED”
–WX-78
“I'm afraid it will not be able to withstand these tectonic shifts forever.”
–Wickerbottom
“It's startin' to look a little unsteady.”
–Woodie
“It's buckling under the weight.”
–Maxwell
“This nöble pillar hath withstööd much för öur sakes.”
–Wigfrid
“Uh oh, those cracks are looking bigger...”
–Webber
“I fear it won't be standing much longer.”
–Warly
“Cracking? Uh oh...”
–Wormwood
“Looks like it could use some repairs.”
–Winona
“Alas and alack, the stone has a crack!”
–Wortox
“Hey, stop it! Not allowed to break, florp!”
–Wurt
“Don't worry Woby, I'm sure it'll be fine!”
–Walter
“Everything breaks down over time. Well, most things anyway.”
–Wanda
“It's all under wraps for now.”
–Wilson
“Hmm. That looks suspiciously like work.”
–Willow
“Do not look! Is not done yet!”
–Wolfgang
“What hides beneath that shroud?”
–Wendy
“LARGE QUANTITIES OF STONE REQUIRED”
–WX-78
“It appears some construction is required.”
–Wickerbottom
“Needs some more work yet, eh?”
–Woodie
“There is work to be done. For someone else.”
–Maxwell
“The building hath cömmenced!”
–Wigfrid
“What's hiding under there?”
–Webber
“What's cooking under there?”
–Warly
“Not done”
–Wormwood
“Looks like there's some buildin' to be done!”
–Winona
“A pile of pillar-in-progress.”
–Wortox
“Need rocks! MORE ROCKS!!”
–Wurt
“I bet there's some interesting secrets hiding under there!”
–Walter
“Oh! I thought that was done already.”
–Wanda
“You were once tall and strong.”
–Wilson
“I guess I'll have to keep an eye out for falling rocks again.”
–Willow
“Is crumbled. Very sad.”
–Wolfgang
“Nothing good can last...”
–Wendy
“DRAT”
–WX-78
“It would be prudent to attempt some repairs.”
–Wickerbottom
“Shucks, I came too late to yell "timbeerrrrr"!”
–Woodie
“It couldn't withstand the pressure.”
–Maxwell
“We shall rebuild!”
–Wigfrid
“Oh no, it broke!”
–Webber
“Crumbled like a dry pastry.”
–Warly
“Fell down”
–Wormwood
“I'd better roll up my sleeves and get to rebuildin'.”
–Winona
“All that trouble for a pile of rubble.”
–Wortox
“Didn't do it!”
–Wurt
“Um. Maybe I spoke too soon...”
–Walter
“Time has not been kind.”
–Wanda

After they've been fully built or repaired, Pillars withstand 10 Earthquakes before losing durability. “Eventually, we all fall.”

“Broken? Perhaps this material is not as indestructible as I surmised.”

–Woodie

“How the mighty have fallen.”

“If at first you don't succeed...”

“Well don't just look at it. Let's get to fixin'.”

–Wortox
“Glorp! Found it like that.”
–Wurt
“How could it be so destructible?”
–Walter
“Does anything last?”
–Wanda

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Cần có các Trụ đỡ được gia cố để bảo vệ khu vực khỏi các vật thể rơi xuống trong trận động đất. Ban đầu, bạn cần chế tạo Giàn giáo Trụ, sau đó đặt 40 Đá vào đó. Sau khi được xây dựng hoặc sửa chữa hoàn chỉnh, Trụ cột chịu được 10 trận động đất trước khi mất đi độ bền. Trụ cột mất đi 1 độ bền cho mỗi trận động đất, nó ngăn không cho các mảnh vụn rơi xuống xung quanh nó, điều này sẽ hiển thị rõ ràng trên Trụ cột dưới dạng các vết nứt lớn. Người chơi có thể lựa chọn sửa chữa nó bằng cách nhét Đá vào đó, với số lượng tùy thuộc vào mức độ hư hỏng của cột. Nếu Shadow Rift hiện đang hoạt động…

If a Shadow Rift is currently active, it will also block [[Cave-in Boulder]]s for no extra cost. When hit by a [[Tools#Hammering Tools|Hammer]], each hit results in 1 Earthquake of durability loss, which means that from full durability, it takes 50 hits to fully break a Support Pillar. {{clear}} == Reinforced Dreadstone Pillar == {{Object Infobox |description = The power of darkness holds up well under pressure. |icon = Dreadstone_Pillar_Scaffold.png |name = Dreadstone Pillar |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Brightshade Smasher}} |image= Dreadstone Pillar 45 Build.png|Full Dreadstone Pillar Scaffold 45.png|Scaffold |ingredient1 = Dreadstone |multiplier1 = 4 {{pic24|Boards}}×2 |upgrade ={{pic24|Dreadstone}}×40 |tier_text = {{Pic32|Blueprint (rare)}} ({{Pic32|Nightmare Werepig}}) |filter = {{Pic32|Structures Filter}} |spawnCode = "support_pillar_dreadstone"

Let's get to fixin'. |warly = If at first you don't succeed... |wortox = The Pillar of Dread looks rather dead, hyuyu! |wormwood = Goodbye, tall rocks |wurt = Glorp! Found it like that. |walter = How could it be so destructible? |wanda = Does anything last? |choose = wortox }} '''Reinforced Dreadstone Pillars''' are obtained by learning one of the blueprints dropped from [[Nightmare Werepig]], after defeating the [[Ancient Guardian]]. Dreadstone Pillars work similarly to Support Pillars, but are much more resilient as they can regenerate their durability every 2 days. It has the same 10 Earthquake grace period before it starts cracking, which is also regenerated over time. Realistically, the player would not need to repair it manually by inserting [[Dreadstone]] into it, as it would regenerate enough while it's not withstanding earthquakes, but if the Dreadstone Pillar is ever damaged enough for it to be destroyed, the player will need to repair it by inserting 40 [[Dreadstone]]s, as in this stage it does not regenerate durability. While the Dreadstone Pillar is in a damaged state and repairing itself, it has a negative [[Sanity]] aura within a radius of 8 wall units, with the intensity of the drain depending on the player's distance to the Pillar. The maximum sanity drain from its aura when standing right next to is around -10{{Sanity}}/min. For the Dreadstone Pillar to be broken by a player, a regular hammer is not sufficient. It can only be broken by a [[Brightshade Smasher]] or [[Woodie/Don't Starve Together|Woodie]] with the {{pic|24|The Werebeaver III}}[[Woodie/Don't Starve Together#The Werebeaver|The Werebeaver III]] Skill. Each hit results in 1 Earthquake of durability loss. From full durability, it takes 50 hits with the Smasher or Werebeaver for the Dreadstone Pillar to break. The Dreadstone Pillar Wreckage left behind when it breaks also requires the aforementioned tools to be destroyed. {{clear}} =={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia== * The Reinforced Support Pillar was introduced in the ''[[Skill Spotlight Update#Wormwood, Wolfgang, Woodie|Skill Spotlight: Wormwood, Wolfgang, Woodie]]'' update. * Before update 567776, they did not have a 90° model, and the Scaffold
